rulings.Case Title: IN RE: PLANT CONSTRUCTION COMPANY, L.P.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Release Of Mechanics' Lien
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 10, 2024 line 1. PETITIONER PLANT CONSTRUCTION COMPANY, L.P.'S NOTICE OF HEARING ON PETITION FOR RELEASE OF MECHANICS' LIEN is OFF CALENDAR. Dismissal on file. =(501/HEK) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: BRYANT FU VS. SHUZHEN TU ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudications
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 10, 2024 line 4. DEFENDANT VI DAN TRAN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATIONS as to the first, second and third causes of action in plaintiff Bryant Fu's second amended complaint are denied. All of Mr. Tran's objections to evidence submitted by Mr. Fu are overruled. As shown in Mr. Fu's amended separate statement and the evidence submitted by Mr. Fu, there are triable issues as to all three of Mr. Fu's claims against Mr. Mr. Tran. rulings.Case Title: PHILIP ACHILLES ET AL VS. PENDER CAPITAL, INC, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 10, 2024 line 6. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT is OFF CALENDAR, moot. The Second Amended Complaint filed in this action is stricken. The Complaint is purportedly filed by counsel making limited scope appearance for Phillip Achilles. There is no indication if this purported appearance is for Mr. Achillies, an individual, Mr. Achilles, as trustee, or both. Furthermore, at the time of the filing of the Second Amended Complaint there was no mandatory form CIV-150 on file indicating that filing of the Second Amended Complaint is within the scope of the limited representation and when this representation is to end. Plaintiff shall have until April 30, 2024 to file an amended pleading in this action. Such pleading must be filed by the attorney of record (current at the time of the filing) or by limited scope counsel after the filing of CIV-150 expressly covering the filing of an amended pleading and indicating when the limited scope representation associated with the filing of the amended pleading is to terminate. CRC 3.36. =(501/HEK) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
